healthcare_standards.name: RxNorm
healthcare_standards.description: RxNorm is a standardized naming system for clinical drugs developed by the U.S. National Library of Medicine. It provides normalized names and unique identifiers for medicines and links different drug vocabularies used across pharmacy and healthcare systems.
purpose: Support e-prescriptions and clinical decision systems
healthcare_standards.example_use_case: Ensures that 'Paracetamol 500 mg Tablet' is recognized as the same drug across hospitals and pharmacies.

healthcare_standards.name: SNOMED CT
healthcare_standards.description: SNOMED CT (Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine – Clinical Terms) is a comprehensive clinical healthcare terminology used to encode diseases, procedures, symptoms, and clinical findings in electronic health records.
purpose: Improve data sharing across healthcare systems
healthcare_standards.example_use_case: Recording 'Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus' using a standard code instead of free text.

healthcare_standards.name: LOINC
healthcare_standards.description: LOINC (Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes) is a universal standard for identifying laboratory tests, clinical measurements, and observations.
purpose: Improve interoperability between labs and hospitals
healthcare_standards.example_use_case: Assigning a standard code to a 'Blood Glucose Test' result.

healthcare_standards.name: ICD
healthcare_standards.description: ICD (International Classification of Diseases) is a global diagnostic classification system developed by the World Health Organization for coding diseases and health conditions.
healthcare_standards.example_use_case: Using ICD-10 code E11 for Type 2 Diabetes diagnosis.

healthcare_standards.name: FHIR
healthcare_standards.description: FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) is a modern healthcare data exchange standard developed by HL7 that uses APIs and JSON/XML formats to share healthcare information electronically.
key_features: Modular resources like Patient, Observation, Medication
healthcare_standards.example_use_case: A mobile health app retrieving patient lab reports from a hospital system.

healthcare_standards.name: Mirth Connect
healthcare_standards.description: Mirth Connect is an open-source healthcare integration engine used to connect different healthcare systems and transform healthcare messages between formats such as HL7, FHIR, and databases.
healthcare_standards.example_use_case: Converting HL7 lab messages into FHIR format for modern applications.

healthcare_standards.name: ABDM
healthcare_standards.description: ABDM (Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission) is India's national digital health ecosystem initiative that enables secure health data exchange using Health IDs, consent-based access, and interoperable standards.
purpose: Create digital health infrastructure in India
healthcare_standards.example_use_case: A patient sharing medical records between two hospitals using ABHA consent.
